title: Advanced Project
summary: Build a production-grade REST API with authentication, database, testing, and deployment config

sections.title: The Full Stack Project — End to End
sections.content: This project combines every advanced concept into a single coherent application: a task management REST API. You will build: Express.js server, SQLite database with better-sqlite3, JWT authentication and middleware, input validation with Zod, unit and integration tests with Vitest, and a Docker deployment configuration. Each feature builds on a previous lesson — SOLID principles for architecture, database skills for persistence, testing patterns for reliability, and deployment knowledge for shipping.

sections.title: JWT authentication middleware
sections.content: Verifies JWT tokens on protected routes and attaches user info to the request

sections.title: Project Structure and Layered Architecture
sections.content: Adopt a professional project structure: src/routes/ (HTTP layer), src/middleware/ (auth, validation, error handling), src/services/ (business logic), src/repositories/ (database access), src/validators/ (Zod schemas), src/config/ (environment and dependencies). Each layer has a single responsibility. Routes never touch the database directly. Repositories never send HTTP responses. Services coordinate between repositories and validators. This structure is the same pattern used in production Node.js applications at scale.

sections.title: Skipping input validation on the server
sections.content: The #1 mistake developers make in API projects is relying on client-side validation alone. Wrong: trusting that the frontend sends valid data — a user can send any request via curl or Postman. Correct: validate every input on the server using a schema library like Zod. Define schemas for each endpoint: const createTaskSchema = z.object({ title: z.string().min(1).max(200), priority: z.enum(['low', 'medium', 'high']) }). Apply validation middleware before route handlers.

sections.title: Your turn
sections.prompt: Build the complete task management API. Implement: (1) POST /auth/register and POST /auth/login returning JWT tokens, (2) CRUD for tasks at /tasks (all require auth middleware), (3) Zod validation for all inputs, (4) integration tests for all endpoints using a test database (SQLite :memory:), (5) error handling middleware that catches all unhandled errors and returns consistent JSON, (6) a Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml for deployment.
hints: How does the test setup create a fresh database before each test and destroy it after?
How do you structure error handling middleware to catch both Zod validation errors and unexpected errors?
What docker-compose services are needed — just the app, or also a database container?
